title: Gentle wool and delicates wash
subtitle: A castile-based liquid wash for wool, silk, and hand-wash fabrics
excerpt: A mild liquid wash for wool, silk, and other delicate fabrics, made with liquid castile soap, white wine vinegar, and lavender essential oil. The castile soap cleans; the vinegar rinse acts as a fabric softener and maintains the slightly acidic pH that woollen fibres prefer.
sourceNotes: Wool fibre chemistry: wool (keratin protein) is damaged by alkaline conditions (high pH strips the protective lanolin and scales the fibre, causing felting). Neutral to slightly acidic wash conditions (pH 6 to 7) protect wool. Castile soap is mildly alkaline; dilution with water and a vinegar rinse brings the final wash pH into the safer range. Standard textile chemistry reference.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 bottle (~250 ml, approximately 15 to 20 wool-wash loads)
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Shake gently before each use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in the bottle at room temperature away from sunlight. Use within 6 months. Shake before each use if the ingredients separate. Discard if an off smell develops.

glossaryTerms.definition: The measure of acidity or alkalinity on a scale from 0 (most acid) to 14 (most alkaline). Wool fibres are protein-based and prefer a slightly acidic wash environment (pH 6 to 7); very alkaline conditions cause the fibre scales to open, leading to felting and shrinkage.

content.text: Wool felts in hot alkaline water: the fibre scales open and lock together permanently. A mild wool wash uses cool water and gentle handling to keep the

of the wash water in the neutral-to-slightly-acidic range wool prefers. Liquid castile soap is diluted (2 tablespoons per 250 ml of water) to keep the alkalinity low. White wine vinegar in the rinse water acts as a fabric softener, lowers the rinse pH, and removes soap residue that can stiffen wool. Lavender essential oil adds a light fragrance that fades through drying. Use 2 tablespoons per basin of cool water for hand-washing, or use in a wool machine cycle at 30°C.

items.prepNote: 40% of formula; unscented castile soap preferred
56% of formula; dilutes the castile soap
3% of formula; adds a slight acidity to protect wool fibres

content.text: Combine all ingredients in the bottle using a funnel if needed. Fit the cap and roll gently to mix. Label with the contents and date. The wash is ready to use immediately.

Fill a basin with cool water (30°C maximum). Add 2 tablespoons of the wool wash and stir gently. Submerge the garment and press it through the water 5 to 10 times without wringing or twisting. Leave to soak for 3 to 5 minutes. Drain and refill with clean cool water for the rinse. For the final rinse, add 2 tablespoons of white wine vinegar to the rinse water; this neutralises any soap residue and softens the fibres. Press out excess water gently; do not wring. Roll in a clean towel to absorb remaining moisture, then reshape and dry flat.

Use the wool or delicates cycle at 30°C or below. Add 3 tablespoons of the wool wash to the detergent drawer. Add 2 tablespoons of white wine vinegar to the fabric softener compartment as a rinse conditioner. Use a low spin speed.
Store at room temperature away from sunlight. Shake before each use. Use within 6 months. Discard if an off smell develops.
